title: 10 best herbal creams for mouth ulcers

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and pain associated with mouth ulcers by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Wound-healing properties: The polysaccharides present in aloe vera, including acemannan, promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue hydration, and enhancing the migration of cells to the affected area.

Antimicrobial activity: Aloe vera's antifungal and antibacterial compounds, such as anthraquinones and phenolic acids, help prevent infection and promote a healthy oral environment by inhibiting the growth of microorganisms that can contribute to mouth ulcer development.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with mouth ulcers because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant contains compounds that reduce swelling and ease pain. When applied to a mouth ulcer, aloe vera cream can help to calm the area and promote healing. Its antibacterial properties also prevent infections from developing, allowing the ulcer to recover quickly and reducing the risk of complications. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to treat mouth ulcers and other oral health issues.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is an anthraquinone glycoside that helps with mouth ulcers by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which reduce pain, swelling, and the risk of infection.

Aloe barbadensis polysaccharides: Aloe barbadensis polysaccharides are complex carbohydrates that help with mouth ulcers by promoting wound healing, reducing inflammation, and enhancing the production of growth factors that repair damaged tissues.

Vitamins (A, C, E): Vitamins A, C, and E in Aloe vera creams help with mouth ulcers by providing antioxidant protection, reducing oxidative stress, and promoting the healing process by enhancing collagen synthesis and tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel from the leaves of Aloe vera is used to make creams for mouth ulcers because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to reduce pain and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of pure aloe vera gel from 2-3 mature aloe vera plants and 50g of coconut oil in a container.
Infuse 20g of dried herbs in 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.
Melt 50g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a double boiler or a heat proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Whip the melted mixture with 10g of shea butter and 10g of vitamin e oil until it thickens and emulsifies.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain associated with mouth ulcers, making them feel more comfortable and allowing for faster healing.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties in pot marigold cream can help prevent infections and promote the growth of healthy tissues, reducing the risk of complications and speeding up the healing process.

Wound-healing: Pot marigold's wound-healing properties contain compounds that stimulate collagen synthesis, improve tissue oxygenation, and enhance fibroblast proliferation, all of which contribute to faster and more effective ulcer healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with mouth ulcers because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in Calendula, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, work together to reduce pain, swelling, and bleeding in the affected area. Additionally, its antibacterial and antifungal properties help prevent infection, promoting a faster healing process. By creating a protective barrier and soothing the tissue, Calendula creams can help alleviate discomfort and speed up the recovery of mouth ulcers.
remedy_constituents: Naphthoquinones: These compounds have been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce pain and promote healing in mouth ulcers.
Carotenoids: Specifically, Calendula extracts contain beta-carotene, a precursor to vitamin A that promotes wound healing, reduces inflammation, and protects against oxidative stress in mouth ulcers.

Triterpene saponins: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help soothe and protect the oral mucosa, reducing discomfort and promoting the healing of mouth ulcers.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used in creams for mouth ulcers due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and protect the affected area.
Leaves: Used in creams for mouth ulcers due to their ability to reduce pain and inflammation, promoting a faster healing process.
Seeds: Used in creams for mouth ulcers due to their potential to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate the condition.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the calendula flowers on a dry sunny day for a high potency extract.
Infuse 100g of dried calendula flowers in 1l of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 4 to 6 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the calendula extract.
Mix 100g of the calendula extract with 100g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter to create a smooth paste.
Add 50ml of distilled water and 20ml of vitamin e oil to the paste and heat it gently to emulsify.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas coneflower contains compounds like triterpenoids and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with mouth ulcers.

Antimicrobial properties: The herbs in Kansas coneflower creams have been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the growth of bacteria and viruses that may contribute to mouth ulcers.

Wound-healing properties: Kansas coneflower has been traditionally used to promote wound healing, and its extracts contain compounds like kaempferol and quercetin that have been shown to enhance collagen synthesis and improve tissue repair, which can help accelerate the healing process of mouth ulcers.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with mouth ulcers because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, help to reduce swelling and promote healing in the affected area. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Echinacea angustifolia cream help to combat bacterial and viral infections that can contribute to the formation of mouth ulcers. This makes it a popular natural remedy for soothing and healing mouth ulcers.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, such as echinacoside, may help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which can aid in the recovery from mouth ulcers.
Alkaloids: Alkaloids like echinamine and echinacin may possess anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, helping to alleviate mouth ulcer pain and accelerate the healing process.
Polyphenols: Polyphenols, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing oxidative stress and promoting the healing of mouth ulcers.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are often used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory and healing properties beneficial for mouth ulcers.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain similar bioactive compounds to the roots, providing a potential alternative for treating mouth ulcers.
Flowers: The flowers are used as they contain some bioactive compounds, although in lower concentrations compared to the roots and leaves, they might still offer some benefits for mouth ulcers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 ounce of echinacea angustifolia roots with a sterile knife in early fall when plants are mature.
Dry the harvested roots in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours to preserve their properties.
Grind 2 teaspoons of dried echinacea roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 2 ounces of beeswax and 2 ounces of coconut oil in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes and pour it into glass jars for storage and later use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with mouth ulcers, promoting faster healing.

Antimicrobial activity: Herbal licorice creams have antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can contribute to mouth ulcers, preventing further infection and promoting recovery.

Soothing demulcent properties: Licorice root has a soothing and protective effect on mucous membranes, providing a barrier against irritants and reducing discomfort associated with mouth ulcers, while also promoting healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with mouth ulcers because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area. By applying a topical cream, these beneficial compounds can directly reach the mouth ulcers, providing quick relief from pain and discomfort. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate inflammation and reduce pain associated with mouth ulcers by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.

Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and protect the oral mucosa, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing of mouth ulcers.

Coumarins: These phenolic compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce the severity of mouth ulcers by preventing bacterial growth and minimizing tissue damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams for mouth ulcers because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams for mouth ulcers because they also contain glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make creams for mouth ulcers because they contain glycosides that have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, providing relief from mouth ulcers.
remedy_recipe: Steep 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 1000 milliliters of hot water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal liquid.
Combine 50 grams of beeswax with 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler over low heat for 15 minutes to melt the beeswax.
Mix the herbal liquid with the melted beeswax mixture and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil to create the cream.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Herbal peppermint creams contain antimicrobial compounds that help combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can contribute to mouth ulcers, reducing their severity and duration.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: The menthol content in peppermint helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with mouth ulcers, providing relief from pain and discomfort.

Soilative Properties: Peppermint's cooling and soothing properties help calm irritated mucous membranes and tissues, promoting a healthy environment for the mouth to heal and reducing the risk of complications.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with mouth ulcers because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The menthol content in peppermint creams provides a cooling sensation that numbs the pain and reduces inflammation in the affected area. This helps to promote healing and reduce discomfort. remedy_parts: Leaves: They are often used to make creams for mouth ulcers due to their high menthol content, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Their essential oils also exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent infection and promote healing in mouth ulcers.
Leaves: The antioxidant properties of the essential oils present in leaves can aid in reducing inflammation and promoting the overall healing process of mouth ulcers.
remedy_recipe: Combine 25g of dried mentha x piperita leaves with 250ml of carrier oil in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Mix 15g of beeswax with 20g of shea butter in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the infused oil to the beeswax mixture, stirring until a smooth consistency is achieved.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Slippery elm cream helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with mouth ulcers, providing relief from pain and discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of ulmus rubra leaves and 50g of its bark under the full moon for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven at 50c for 2 hours to preserve its properties.
Infuse the dried ulmus rubra in 500ml of carrier oil for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal extract.
Mix the herbal extract with beeswax and a preservative in a 3:1:1 ratio and whip until smooth.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried curcuma longa rhizomes from a trusted source for their medicinal properties.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 50g of the ground powder with 200g of a carrier oil such as coconut oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes and then heat it gently for 30 minutes to facilitate absorption.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and bottle the resulting herbal cream for storage purposes.

remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of ginger in mouth ulcer creams due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, particularly gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties.

remedy_recipe: Extract 3 grams of dried zingiber officinale root in a 2-ounce glass jar with 4 ounces of 80-proof vodka for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids after 2 weeks of infusion.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Combine the strained liquid with 2 tablespoons of shea butter and 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a saucepan over low heat.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer for 5 minutes until it thickens and forms a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antimicrobial Properties: Helps combat bacterial infections that can cause or exacerbate mouth ulcers, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 5 tablespoons of melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers from mature plants at peak oil production time.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours.
Steep 1 cup of dried plant material in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids then add 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of beeswax.
Blend the mixture in a double boiler at 180 degrees fahrenheit until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches a smooth consistency.

Leaves: The leaves of Matricaria chamomilla are used to make creams for mouth ulcers because of their antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers on a dry morning to ensure highest quality.
Dry the harvested flowers for 2 weeks in a warm dark room to remove excess moisture.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes to create tea.
Mix 1 cup of cold cream with 2 tablespoons of matricaria chamomilla tea and 2 teaspoons of beeswax to combine ingredients.
Heat the cream mixture in a double boiler for 10 minutes to melt beeswax and create a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with mouth ulcers because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest dried goldenseal root powder from a reliable source, measuring out two teaspoons for the recipe.
Combine the powdered goldenseal with one teaspoon of beeswax and half teaspoon of coconut oil in a small mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax has fully melted and a smooth consistency forms after 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in one tablespoon of aloe vera gel to enhance skin soothing properties, allowing it to cool for five minutes.
Pour the cooled cream into a sterilized glass jar and store it in the refrigerator at 40 degrees fahrenheit or below, using within six months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried marshmallow root althaea officinalis weighing approximately 20 grams.
Combine harvested marshmallow root with distilled water in a heat-resistant container at ratio of 1:5.
Bring mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Strain liquid from plant material using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into clean glass containers.
Mix strained liquid with beeswax and coconut oil in proportions of 10 grams liquid to 2 grams wax and 1 gram oil.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves in the morning and wash them gently with distilled water.
Dry the harvested plantago major leaves in a warm, dry place for 3-4 hours or until crumbly.
Crush the dried plantago major leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of the crushed plantago major powder with 100 milliliters of coconut oil and 10 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture slowly over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 30-45 minutes or until the beeswax melts and the cream thickens.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with mouth ulcers because they possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the affected area. remedy_recipe: Gather fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers in a clean container for later use.
Steep one tablespoon of dried hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers in two cups boiling water for 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl to collect the herbal tea.
Mix the cooled herbal tea with three tablespoons of beeswax and six tablespoons of coconut oil, stirring until melted.
Pour the mixture into small glass jars and refrigerate for at least two hours or overnight before use.

Wound-healing properties: Red clover's triterpenoid saponins have been shown to accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue strength, and enhancing epithelialization.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, these parts have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, making them effective against bacterial and viral infections that cause mouth ulcers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh trifolium pratense flowers in the morning for optimal potency and store them in an airtight container.
Combine 200 grams of dried trifolium pratense flowers with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, then add 20 grams of beeswax and heat the mixture until melted.
Pour the cream into sterilized tubes or jars, allowing it to cool and solidify before use as a topical treatment for mouth ulcers within 3 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with mouth ulcers because of its anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties. The herb's allantoin content promotes tissue regeneration, reducing pain and discomfort caused by mouth ulcers. Additionally, comfrey's antimicrobial properties help to prevent infection, promoting a healthy environment for ulcer healing. By applying comfrey cream directly to the affected area, it helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, accelerating the recovery process and reducing the risk of scarring.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Comfrey cream's allantoin content helps to accelerate wound healing by promoting cell proliferation, differentiation, and migration, which accelerates the repair of damaged oral mucosa.
Polysaccharides: The polysaccharide component in comfrey cream has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain associated with mouth ulcers, making them more comfortable for patients.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound found in comfrey, exhibits antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help to prevent infection and promote healthy wound healing in the oral cavity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in allantoin, a natural soothing agent that helps to accelerate healing and reduce pain associated with mouth ulcers.
Leaves: Containing rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and discomfort caused by mouth sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh symphytum officinale leaves from the field or purchase dried ones from a reputable supplier weighing about 100g.
Chop the harvested leaves into small pieces and soak them in one liter of boiling water for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to obtain the herbal extract discarding the solids and measuring the volume to about 500ml.
Mix the herbal extract with 100g of coconut oil and 20g of beeswax in a heat-resistant glass bowl stirring until fully melted and well combined.
Pour the mixture into sterilized tubes or jars allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature for at least two hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with mouth ulcers because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as dandelion root and chamomile, help to reduce swelling and soothe irritated mucous membranes. Additionally, the antibacterial and antifungal properties of the herbs work together to prevent infection and promote healing. This natural approach can provide quick relief from pain, discomfort, and inflammation associated with mouth ulcers, making it a popular alternative treatment for oral health concerns.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and pain associated with mouth ulcers, promoting a quicker recovery.
Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of dandelion cream inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause mouth ulcers, preventing infection and speeding up healing.

Wound-healing properties: Dandelion's rich supply of vitamins A, C, and K, as well as its antioxidant content, promote wound healing by stimulating collagen production, improving tissue strength, and protecting against oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and healing mouth ulcers.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids that provide antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties to help combat bacterial infections and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots weighing approximately 100 grams then clean thoroughly.
Grind the dried and chopped roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered root mixture with 250 milliliters of distilled water and let it steep at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Filter the liquid extract through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to obtain a clear decoction, then mix with 20 grams of petroleum jelly and 10 grams of beeswax.
Stir the mixture in a double boiler until the ingredients are fully incorporated and the cream reaches a smooth consistency after about 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with mouth ulcers because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the affected area. The cream's ability to reduce swelling and redness provides rapid relief from pain and discomfort. Additionally, yarrow's antimicrobial qualities help combat bacterial infections that may contribute to mouth ulcer formation. By promoting a healthy environment, yarrow creams accelerate the healing process, leaving the mouth feeling smooth and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Yarrow's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can contribute to the development of mouth ulcers, promoting a faster healing process.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with mouth ulcers, creating a more conducive environment for healing.

Wound-healing stimulation: Yarrow's stimulatory properties can enhance collagen synthesis, promote blood flow, and accelerate wound closure, helping to speed up the recovery process from mouth ulcers.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and heal mouth ulcers.
Buds: The buds are utilized due to their rich content of flavonoids, which possess antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects that aid in reducing pain and inflammation caused by mouth ulcers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves, flowers, or stems in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried achillea millefolium into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, 1 tablespoon of beeswax, and 2 drops of peppermint essential oil in a double boiler until melted and smooth.
Remove from heat, pour mixture into glass containers, and let cool at room temperature for 30 minutes to set.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for mouth ulcers are topical ointments containing plant extracts that help soothe and heal mouth ulcers. These creams are made from natural ingredients such as aloe vera, calendula, and tea tree oil, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Examples of herbal creams include Aloe Vera Gel and Calendula Cream. They provide relief from pain and discomfort, allowing individuals to eat and speak comfortably, and significantly improve their quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to mouth ulcers that are treated with herbal creams are skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and psoriasis. These creams often contain soothing herbs like aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil, which help calm inflammation, reduce redness, and promote healing. Additionally, herbal creams may be used to treat burns, wounds, and minor cuts, providing relief from discomfort and promoting tissue repair.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with mouth ulcers is a blend of aloe vera, calendula, and peppermint. Aloe vera soothes and calms the affected area, reducing inflammation and pain. Calendula accelerates the healing process by promoting tissue repair and regeneration. Meanwhile, peppermint provides a cooling sensation and helps to reduce discomfort. By applying a combination of these creams, you can effectively alleviate the symptoms of mouth ulcers and promote quick recovery. Regular use can also prevent future occurrences.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, herbal creams derived from M. communis have been shown to be effective in treating mouth ulcers due to their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and wound-healing effects. The study, which extracted information from various resources including traditional texts and modern clinical trials, found that M. communis essential oil and decoctions significantly reduced the average time of pain relief and decreased the size of ulcers in patients with minor Recurrent Aphthous Stomatitis (RAS). In fact, 81% of patients were satisfied with the use of M. communis topical decoctions, making it a promising natural remedy for mouth ulcers.
